back_translation: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,

Command Aaron and his sons, saying: This is the law of the burnt offering. The burnt offering shall be on the hearth on the altar all night until morning, and the fire of the altar shall be kept burning on it.

And the priest shall wear his linen robe, and he shall wear linen undergarments on his flesh, and he shall remove the ashes of what the fire has consumed of the burnt offering on the altar, and he shall place them beside the altar.
Then he shall take off his garments and wear other garments, and he shall carry the ashes outside the camp to a clean place.

And the fire on the altar shall be kept burning on it; it shall not be extinguished. And the priest shall add wood to it every morning, and he shall arrange the burnt offering on it, and he shall burn as smoke on it the fat of the peace offerings.
Fire shall be kept burning continually on the altar; it shall not be extinguished.
And this is the law of the grain offering: Aaron's sons shall present it before the face of the Lord in front of the altar.

And he shall take from it with his handful from the fine flour of the grain offering and from its oil, together with all the frankincense that is on the grain offering, and he shall burn it as smoke on the altar, a pleasing aroma, its memorial portion, to the Lord.
And the remainder of it, Aaron and his sons shall eat. It shall be eaten unleavened in a holy place, in the courtyard of the tent of meeting they shall eat it.
It shall not be baked with leaven; their portion I have given it from My offerings by fire. It is most holy, like the sin offering and like the guilt offering.
Every male among the sons of Aaron shall eat it. It is a permanent statute for your generations from the offerings by fire to the Lord. Anything that touches them shall become holy.
And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,

This is the offering of Aaron and his sons that they shall present to the Lord on the day he is anointed: a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a regular grain offering, half of it in the morning and half of it in the evening.
On a flat griddle in oil it shall be prepared, well-kneaded you shall bring it, baked pieces of the grain offering broken in pieces, you shall present a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
And the anointed priest who succeeds him from his sons shall prepare it. It is a permanent statute for the Lord; it shall be burned entirely as smoke.
And every grain offering of a priest shall be entirely burned; it shall not be eaten.
And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,

Speak to Aaron and to his sons, saying: This is the law of the sin offering. In the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered, the sin offering shall be slaughtered before the face of the Lord. It is most holy.
The priest who offers it as a sin offering shall eat it; in a holy place it shall be eaten, in the courtyard of the tent of meeting.
Anything that touches its flesh shall become holy, and that which spatters from its blood on a garment that it spatters on, you shall wash in a holy place.
And a clay vessel in which it is boiled shall be broken; and if it is boiled in a bronze vessel, then it shall be scoured and rinsed in water.
Every male among the priests shall eat it; it is most holy.
And any sin offering whose blood is brought into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the holy place shall not be eaten; it shall be burned in fire.
